# CensorZero: Independent Audit of IMI's Assessment of Ukrinform

> This is an open-source research project analyzing 51,589 Ukrinform articles across two periods to verify claims made by the Institute of Mass Information (IMI) when excluding Ukrinform from its White List.

## Key Facts

- Author: Oleksiy Matsuka, former Director General of Ukrinform (Nov 2023 – May 2024), founder of CensorZero
- Corpus: 51,589 audited articles (51,667 collected from public sitemaps)
- Period 1 (Matsuka era): Nov 9, 2023 – Apr 25, 2024 (26,342 audited articles)
- Period 2 (after departure): Jul 1, 2025 – Dec 15, 2025 (25,247 audited articles)
- IMI excluded Ukrinform on April 26, 2024 citing "parket messages" and "insufficient balance"
- Ukrinform returned to White List on December 16, 2025

## Main Finding

Without military bulletins (39,230 articles): parket rate P1 6.61% vs P2 6.01% (p=0.007, Cohen's d=0.025 — negligible effect)
With military bulletins (51,589 articles): parket rate P1 6.51% vs P2 6.42% (p=0.678 — not significant)

The data do not support the claim that content quality deteriorated under Matsuka's leadership.

## What This Research Checks

- Source count per article (official vs non-official)
- "Parket" content proxy: official source + ≤1 source + 0 non-official sources
- Balance risk: official source + 0 non-official sources

## What This Research Does NOT Check

- Completeness (background, context)
- Factual accuracy
- Fact/comment separation
- Sexism / hate speech
- Sponsored content marking

## Methodology Note

IMI's methodology (published Oct 28, 2022) allows balance to be provided within 12 hours across multiple articles. This analysis applies a stricter criterion — balance within each individual article. Even under this stricter standard, the difference between periods is negligible.

## Links

- Dashboard: https://ukrinform.censorzero.com/
- English version: https://ukrinform.censorzero.com/en.html
- Explorer (all articles): https://ukrinform.censorzero.com/explorer.html
- GitHub: https://github.com/alexmazuka/ukrinform
- Data (CSV): https://github.com/alexmazuka/ukrinform/blob/main/data/corpus_fast.csv
